zh.Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Wotton於大約1325年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親John de Wotton24歲，母親Engaret Dymark36歲。他於1340年與Julian le Prouz在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1373年之前在英國戴文維德沃希。

zh-hans.Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Wotton于大约1325年出生在英国戴文时，他的父亲John de Wotton24岁，母亲Engaret Dymark36岁。他于1340年与Julian le Prouz在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1373年之前在英国戴文維德沃希。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Wooten
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called with Old English wid(u), wudu ‘wood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, such as Wootton in Northamptonshire or Oxfordshire, Wootton Bassett in Wiltshire, Wotton in Surrey, and Wotton under Edge in Gloucestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Wotton was born about 1325, in Devon, England, his father, John de Wotton, was 24 and his mother, Engaret Dymark, was 36. He married Julian le Prouz in 1340, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died before 1373, in Widworthy, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Wotton est né environ 1325, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, John de Wotton, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Engaret Dymark, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Julian le Prouz en 1340, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé avant 1373, à Widworthy, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Wotton um 1325 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Wotton 24 und seine Mutter Engaret Dymark 36. Er hat 1340 in Devon, England Julian le Prouz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 1373 in Widworthy, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Wotton è nato il circa 1325 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, John de Wotton, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Engaret Dymark, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Julian le Prouz nel 1340, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì prima del 1373, nel Widworthy, Devon, England.

ja.1343: から1343年1月9日まで1344年1月8日
ja.Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストル
ja.Widworthy, Devon, England: イギリスデボンウィドウオルシー
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Wottonが約1325年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父John de Wottonは24歳，母Engaret Dymarkは36歳であった。1340年にイギリスデボンでJulian le Prouzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1373年以前にイギリスデボンウィドウオルシーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Wotton(이)가 대략 1325년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Wotton(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Engaret Dymark(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1340년 영국 데폰에서 Julian le Prouz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1373년 이전 영국 데폰 위드워시에서 사망했다.

pt.1343: de 9 de janeiro de 1343 a 8 de janeiro de 1344
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Wotton nasceu aproximadamente 1325, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Wotton, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Engaret Dymark, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Julian le Prouz em 1340, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu antes 1373, em Widworthy, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Wotton родился около 1325 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Wotton было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Engaret Dymark, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Julian le Prouz в 1340 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер перед 1373 г. в местности видворти, девон, Англия.

es.1343: desde 9 de enero de 1343 hasta 8 de enero de 1344
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Wotton nació aproximadamente 1325, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Wotton, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Engaret Dymark, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Julian le Prouz en 1340, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió antes de 1373, en Widworthy, Devon, Inglaterra.
